title: eriogonum thompsoniae var. albiflorum
intro: Eriogonum thompsoniae var. albiflorum, also known as White Sulphur Buckwheat, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This shrubby perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum thompsoniae var. albiflorum include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in some traditional skincare products to harness its potential benefits.
